targetTranslation: The one who (connects to خَلَقَ - the one who created)
ayahArabic: ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ سَبْعَ سَمٰوٰتٍ طِبَاقًا مَّا تَرَىٰ فِى خَلْقِ ٱلرَّحْمٰنِ مِن تَفٰوُتٍ فَٱرْجِعِ ٱلْبَصَرَ هَلْ تَرَىٰ مِن فُطُورٍ
ayahEnglish: [And] who created seven heavens in layers. You do not see in the creation of the Most Merciful any inconsistency. So return [your] vision [to the sky]; do you see any breaks?

targetTranslation: return! (command to one person) (followed by ٱلْبَصَرَ - your vision)
ayahArabic: ثُمَّ ٱرْجِعِ ٱلْبَصَرَ كَرَّتَيْنِ يَنقَلِبْ إِلَيْكَ ٱلْبَصَرُ خَاسِئًا وَهُوَ حَسِيرٌ
ayahEnglish: Then return [your] vision twice again. [Your] vision will return to you humbled while it is fatigued.

targetTranslation: we beautified intensely (subject of the verb that describes what Allah did to the sky)
ayahArabic: وَلَقَدْ زَيَّنَّا ٱلسَّمَآءَ ٱلدُّنْيَا بِمَصٰبِيحَ وَجَعَلْنٰهَا رُجُومًا لِّلشَّيٰطِينِ وَأَعْتَدْنَا لَهُمْ عَذَابَ ٱلسَّعِيرِ
ayahEnglish: And We have certainly beautified the nearest heaven with lamps [i.e., stars] and have made [from] them what is thrown at the devils and have prepared for them the punishment of the Blaze.

targetTranslation: as things thrown (to drive away devils) (object of جَعَلْنٰهَا)

ayahEnglish: And We have certainly beautified the nearest heaven with lamps [i.e., stars] and have made [from] them what is thrown at the devils and have prepared for them the punishment of the Blaze.

targetTranslation: punishment (possessed by السَّعِيرِ - the blazing fire)

ayahEnglish: And We have certainly beautified the nearest heaven with lamps [i.e., stars] and have made [from] them what is thrown at the devils and have prepared for them the punishment of the Blaze.

ayahArabic: وَلِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ بِرَبِّهِمْ عَذَابُ جَهَنَّمَ وَبِئْسَ ٱلْمَصِيرُ
ayahEnglish: And for those who disbelieved in their Lord is the punishment of Hell, and wretched is the destination.

ayahArabic: وَلِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ بِرَبِّهِمْ عَذَابُ جَهَنَّمَ وَبِئْسَ ٱلْمَصِيرُ
ayahEnglish: And for those who disbelieved in their Lord is the punishment of Hell, and wretched is the destination.

ayahEnglish: And for those who disbelieved in their Lord is the punishment of Hell, and wretched is the destination.

targetTranslation: when (introducing what happens when they are thrown)

ayahEnglish: When they are thrown into it, they hear from it a [dreadful] inhaling while it boils up.

ayahArabic: تَكَادُ تَمَيَّزُ مِنَ ٱلْغَيْظِ كُلَّمَآ أُلْقِىَ فِيهَا فَوْجٌ سَأَلَهُمْ خَزَنَتُهَآ أَلَمْ يَأْتِكُمْ نَذِيرٌ
ayahEnglish: It almost bursts with rage. Every time a company is thrown into it, its keepers ask them, "Did there not come to you a warner?"

targetTranslation: they said (referring to the disbelievers speaking to the angels)
ayahArabic: قَالُوا۟ بَلَىٰ قَدْ جَآءَنَا نَذِيرٌ فَكَذَّبْنَا وَقُلْنَا مَا نَزَّلَ ٱللَّهُ مِن شَىْءٍ إِنْ أَنتُمْ إِلَّا فِى ضَلٰلٍ كَبِيرٍ
ayahEnglish: They will say, "Yes, a warner had come to us, but we denied and said, 'Allāh has not sent down anything. You are not but in great error.'"

ayahEnglish: And they will say, "If only we had been listening or reasoning, we would not be among the companions of the Blaze."

ayahArabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَخْشَوْنَ رَبَّهُم بِٱلْغَيْبِ لَهُم مَّغْفِرَةٌ وَأَجْرٌ كَبِيرٌ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, those who fear their Lord unseen will have forgiveness and great reward.

ayahArabic: وَأَسِرُّوا۟ قَوْلَكُمْ أَوِ ٱجْهَرُوا۟ بِهِۦٓ إِنَّهُۥ عَلِيمٌۢ بِذَاتِ ٱلصُّدُورِ
ayahEnglish: And conceal your speech or publicize it; indeed, He is Knowing of that within the breasts.

ayahArabic: أَلَا يَعْلَمُ مَنْ خَلَقَ وَهُوَ ٱللَّطِيفُ ٱلْخَبِيرُ
ayahEnglish: Does He who created not know, while He is the Subtle, the Aware?

ayahEnglish: Does He who created not know, while He is the Subtle, the Aware?

ayahArabic: هُوَ ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلْأَرْضَ ذَلُولًا فَٱمْشُوا۟ فِى مَنَاكِبِهَا وَكُلُوا۟ مِن رِّزْقِهِۦ وَإِلَيْهِ ٱلنُّشُورُ
ayahEnglish: It is He who made the earth tame for you - so walk among its slopes and eat of His provision - and to Him is the resurrection.

ayahArabic: ءَأَمِنتُم مَّن فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ أَن يَخْسِفَ بِكُمُ ٱلْأَرْضَ فَإِذَا هِىَ تَمُورُ
ayahEnglish: Do you feel secure that He who is above would not cause the earth to swallow you and suddenly it would sway?

targetTranslation: who/whoever (emphasized, asking about the subject of يَخْسِفَ)

ayahEnglish: Do you feel secure that He who is above would not cause the earth to swallow you and suddenly it would sway?

targetTranslation: you all felt safe (asking a group in the past)
ayahArabic: أَمْ أَمِنتُم مَّن فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ أَن يُرْسِلَ عَلَيْكُمْ حَاصِبًا فَسَتَعْلَمُونَ كَيْفَ نَذِيرِ
ayahEnglish: Or do you feel secure that He who is above would not send against you a storm of stones? Then you would know how [severe] was My warning.

ayahArabic: وَلَقَدْ كَذَّبَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ فَكَيْفَ كَانَ نَكِيرِ
ayahEnglish: And already had those before them denied, and how [terrible] was My reproach.

targetTranslation: those who (refers to those who denied before them)

ayahEnglish: And already had those before them denied, and how [terrible] was My reproach.

ayahArabic: أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ إِلَى ٱلطَّيْرِ فَوْقَهُمْ صٰٓفَّاتٍ وَيَقْبِضْنَ مَا يُمْسِكُهُنَّ إِلَّا ٱلرَّحْمٰنُ إِنَّهُۥ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍۭ بَصِيرٌ
ayahEnglish: Do they not see the birds above them with wings outspread and [sometimes] folded in? None holds them [aloft] except the Most Merciful. Indeed He is, of all things, Seeing.

targetTranslation: they see (looking at الطَّيْرِ - the birds)

ayahArabic: قُلْ هُوَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَنشَأَكُمْ وَجَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلسَّمْعَ وَٱلْأَبْصٰرَ وَٱلْأَفْـِٔدَةَ قَلِيلًا مَّا تَشْكُرُونَ
ayahEnglish: Say, "It is He who has produced you and made for you hearing and vision and hearts [i.e., intellect]; little are you grateful."

ayahArabic: فَلَمَّا رَأَوْهُ زُلْفَةً سِيٓـَٔتْ وُجُوهُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَقِيلَ هٰذَا ٱلَّذِى كُنتُم بِهِۦ تَدَّعُونَ

ayahArabic: فَلَمَّا رَأَوْهُ زُلْفَةً سِيٓـَٔتْ وُجُوهُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَقِيلَ هٰذَا ٱلَّذِى كُنتُم بِهِۦ تَدَّعُونَ

ayahArabic: قُلْ أَرَءَيْتُمْ إِنْ أَهْلَكَنِىَ ٱللَّهُ وَمَن مَّعِىَ أَوْ رَحِمَنَا فَمَن يُجِيرُ ٱلْكٰفِرِينَ مِنْ عَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ
ayahEnglish: Say, [O Muḥammad], "Have you considered: whether Allāh should cause my death and those with me or have mercy upon us, who can protect the disbelievers from a painful punishment?"

ayahArabic: قُلْ هُوَ ٱلرَّحْمٰنُ ءَامَنَّا بِهِۦ وَعَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْنَا فَسَتَعْلَمُونَ مَنْ هُوَ فِى ضَلٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ
ayahEnglish: Say, "He is the Most Merciful; we have believed in Him, and upon Him we have relied. And you will [come to] know who it is that is in clear error."

ayahArabic: قُلْ أَرَءَيْتُمْ إِنْ أَصْبَحَ مَآؤُكُمْ غَوْرًا فَمَن يَأْتِيكُم بِمَآءٍ مَّعِينٍۭ
ayahEnglish: Say, "Have you considered: if your water was to become sunken [into the earth], then who could bring you flowing water?"
